payload.title: Add endless session goto and back commands for tmux session navigation with a back-stack
payload.description: Add 'endless session goto <task|session>' to switch tmux focus to the target session's pane (a task id resolves to the live session working it), and 'endless session back' to return via a browser-style back-stack pushed on each goto. Makes the session-next view's in-flight rows directly navigable.

fields.text:
# endless session goto / back — tmux session navigation

## Goal
Make any session (notably the session-next view's `⟳` in-flight rows) directly navigable:
- `endless session goto <task|session>` — switch tmux focus to the target session's pane.
- `endless session back` — return to where you came from (browser-style).

## `session goto <id>`
- `<id>`: a task id (`E-NNNN` or `NNNN`) or a session id.
- Resolve to a target tmux pane:
  - session id → that session's `sessions.process` (the pane, e.g. `%214`).
  - task id → the live session whose `active_task_id` = that task; if several, the most-recently-active (`last_activity DESC`); if none live, error: "no live session on E-NNNN".
- Push the CURRENT pane (`$TMUX_PANE`) onto the back-stack BEFORE switching.
- Switch with `tmux switch-client -t <pane>` (tmux resolves pane → its window/session). Friendly error if not inside tmux, or if the target pane no longer exists.

## `session back`
- Pop the top of the back-stack and `tmux switch-client -t <pane>`.
- Empty stack → message: "no previous session".
- If the popped pane is gone, drop it and pop the next.

## Back-stack
- A per-tmux-server stack of pane ids. Push on `goto`, pop on `back`.
- Storage: a tmux user option (e.g. `@endless_session_backstack`, space-separated) — simplest, dies with the server — or a small state file under `~/.cache/endless/`. Implementer's choice.

## Implementation notes
- Go (reads-in-Go rule); both verbs slot into the existing `endless session` command group.
- Reuse the session-resolution that `endless session cd` (E-990) already does; here we need session→pane via `sessions.process` rather than session→worktree path.
- Guard rails: not-in-tmux → friendly error; stale/closed target pane → error and drop it from the stack.

## Out of scope
- The `session next` view itself (E-1465) — separate task.

# Implement `endless session next` read command

Reframed from the original You/Agent-primary description to **action-enum primary** (You/Agent kept as a derived secondary actor tag). Full design lives in the epic **E-1461**; this task is the one-shot read command only.

## Scope
Render the live per-session what's-next view from {sessions, session_tasks, task_deps, tasks}:
- **Row set**: union of touched (`session_tasks`) ∪ in-flow (`subtree(active_epic_id)`) ∪ elsewhere (other live sessions' `active_task_id` — cross-session IN FLIGHT, new this round).
- **Next-Action CASE** (see E-1461): IN FLIGHT / ACTIVE / SPAWN / PLAN / VERIFY / STALE / DECIDE / LATER; blocker as a `⛒` decoration on any row; status canonicalized in-query.
- **Compact ≤2-line header**: FROM (lineage), BLOCKS (impact), reserved placeholder, action counts, uptime.
- **1-line-per-task body**: manual-width alignment so the title never widens the action / ID columns; top-N + overflow tally.
- **Output**: human table now (manual-width / `text/tabwriter`); `--json` / `--llm` later.
- **`--all` flag**: by default the view omits done-work (terminal-status touched tasks) — the "remaining-work briefing" intent. `--all` includes everything in `session_tasks` (done tasks shown dimmed/struck).

## Dependencies
- `blocked_by` **E-1462** — `session_tasks.relation` column.
- `blocked_by` **E-1128** — spawn lineage for the FROM header line.
- Reads `active_epic_id` (the epic is the durable goal); does NOT use `goal_task_id` / E-1483.

## Out of scope
- `--watch` / TUI (E-859 / E-1622).
- 2-line title record (future follow-up Mike files if needed).
- Auto-spawning the ready queue (E-1136).

# E-1681 — `endless session goto` / `session back` (tmux session navigation)

## Context

The `session next` view (E-1465) lists in-flight sessions as `⟳` rows keyed by the
task each is working, but there's no way to jump tmux focus to one, and a spawned
session's only way "home" is the literal `tmux switch-client -t %NNN` baked into
its handoff. This task adds:

- `endless session goto <task|session>` — switch the tmux client's focus to the
  target session's pane.
- `endless session back` — return browser-style via a back-stack.

## Design (decided with Mike)

Two concerns were separated:
1. **Back-stack** (drives `back`) — ephemeral, server-life navigation state. **In
   scope here.**
2. **Durable navigation trail** (analysis / recovering a lost session, captures
   manual moves too) — **out of scope; filed as a follow-up** (`--cleans-up E-1681`).

### Back-stack
- **Storage:** a **tmux server option**, keyed per attached client:
  `@endless_backstack_<sanitized client_name>` (value = space-separated tokens,
  top of stack = last token). Falls back to a global `@endless_backstack` when no
  client name is resolvable. Lifetime matches the tmux server, which is exactly as
  long as the pane ids inside it are valid. No file, no DB, no schema change.
  (Sessions writes are Go-only and tmux options are already read/written from
  Python — `@endless_spawned_by`, `@endless_session_uuid` — so this is the
  least-machinery right-sized tool for ephemeral nav state.)
- **Unit = endless session id**, not raw pane (Mike's point: panes are volatile,
  a spawner can restart into a new pane under the same session id). A stack token
  is a session id (digits) resolved to its *current* live pane at pop-time. A
  source location that isn't a tracked session (e.g. a plain shell pane) is stored
  as a raw pane id (`%NNN`) and used directly — the leading `%` distinguishes the
  two token kinds.
- **Scope = the navigator = the attached tmux client.** For a single-terminal
  setup this coincides with "global per tmux server."
- **Capture = `goto` only** (predictable: `back` undoes your last `goto`; manual
  `select-window` moves don't touch it — capturing those belongs to the durable
  trail follow-up).

### `session goto <ref>`
1. Require tmux (`$TMUX`); else friendly error.
2. Resolve `<ref>` → target session → its live pane (rules below); friendly error
   on no-match / ambiguous / no-live-session.
3. Resolve the current pane (`$TMUX_PANE`) → its session id if tracked, else keep
   the raw pane id; push that token onto this client's stack.
4. `tmux switch-client -t <target-pane>`. If the target pane is gone, error and
   pop the token we just pushed (don't leave it dangling).
5. Status to stderr: `• goto E-NNNN → session E-MM (pane %214)`.

Ref resolution (reusing the Go-backed `_live_sessions` read — no new Go):
- `E-NNNN` → task id → live sessions with that `active_task_id`, most recent
  (`last_activity DESC`, already the Go order); none → "no live session on E-NNNN".
- bare digits `N` → if a live session has `endless_session_id == N` **xor** a live
  session has `active_task_id == N`, use the unique match; if **both** match
  different sessions → ambiguous error telling the user to write `E-N` for the task
  (Mike confirmed: ambiguous → error).
- non-numeric → treat as a session ref: prefix-match `harness_session_id` (uuid),
  like `session cd`.
- session ref → that session's `pane_id` (its `sessions.process`).

### `session back`
1. Require tmux; resolve this client's stack.
2. Pop the top token:
   - `%pane` token → use directly; if the pane no longer exists, drop and pop next.
   - session-id token → resolve to that session's *current* live pane; if the
     session is no longer live, drop and pop next.
3. If the stack drains → **spawn-back fallback**: read this window's
   `@endless_spawned_by` (set at spawn = spawner's session id; `task_cmd.py:4441`)
   and switch to that session's current live pane. Live re-resolution is a strict
   superset of seeding (handles a spawner restarting into a new pane) with zero
   change to the spawn path.
4. `tmux switch-client -t <pane>`.
5. Nothing left and no live spawner → message "no previous session".

## Files

- `src/endless/cli.py` (~line 880, in the `session` group) — register
  `@session_cmd.command("goto")` (one positional `target_ref`) and
  `@session_cmd.command("back")`, each delegating into `session_cmd.py`.
- `src/endless/session_cmd.py` — add `session_goto(target_ref)`, `session_back()`,
  and helpers:
  - `_resolve_goto_target(ref, live)` → `(target_pane, label)`.
  - `_backstack_key()` (client name → sanitized option suffix),
    `_backstack_read()/_write()/_push()/_pop()` (tmux option get/set).
  - `_tmux_switch_client(pane)`, `_pane_exists(pane)` (subprocess wrappers,
    alongside existing `_tmux_window_pane_ids`).
  - `_session_pane(live, session_id)` (session id → current live pane),
    `_spawner_pane(live)` (read `@endless_spawned_by` → live pane).
  - Reuse `_live_sessions`, `_project_root_from_cwd`-style resolution as `session
    cd` does.

## Tests — `tests/test_session_goto_back.py` (model: `tests/test_session_cd.py`)

Mock `_live_sessions` + `subprocess.run` (tmux), assert the exact tmux commands:
- goto by task id → pushes current session token, switches to the live session's pane.
- goto by session id / uuid prefix → switches to that pane.
- goto unknown / task with no live session → friendly error, no switch, nothing pushed.
- goto ambiguous bare number → error naming `E-N`, no switch.
- back pops the pushed token (session id → current pane) and switches.
- back empty stack but `@endless_spawned_by` set → switches to spawner's pane.
- back empty stack, no live spawner → "no previous session".
- back drops a stale token (dead pane / ended session) and pops the next.
- both verbs outside tmux → friendly error.

## Verification (end-to-end)
1. `just build` in the worktree (current candidate `bin/endless-go`).
2. `just test` — new suite green, no regressions.
3. Manual, inside tmux from the worktree (sandbox-routed): `goto` a live
   task/session → focus moves; `back` → returns; from a spawned session's pane,
   `back` with empty stack → returns to the spawner; `back` from a fresh pane →
   "no previous session".

## Out of scope (follow-up filed)
- Durable navigation trail (DB, captures manual moves via tmux hooks, queryable
  for usability analysis / finding lost sessions) — separate task, `--cleans-up E-1681`.
- The `session next` view itself (E-1465).

payload.title: Record durable session-navigation trail (manual + goto) for analysis and lost-session recovery
payload.description: Follow-up surfaced while designing E-1681 (goto/back). The E-1681 back-stack is intentionally ephemeral (tmux option, goto-only). Add a separate DURABLE navigation trail: record every focus change — manual select-window/switch-client (via tmux client-session-changed / after-select-window hooks) and 'session goto' — into a mutable runtime DB table keyed by client/session, with a Go write path. Purpose: usability analysis of navigation, and recovering a session a user lost track of. Decoupled from 'session back', which stays goto-only and predictable.

payload.title: Add session-scoped task order (session_tasks.order) and a Claude command to set do/plan sequence
payload.description: Add a per-session implementation order so session next shows do/plan tasks in the sequence to work them, with parallel groups. Claude sets it via a command taking a compact spec where whitespace advances the order and | marks parallel (same order) — e.g. 'E-100 E-101|E-102 E-103' yields 1,2,2,3. Stored as session_tasks.order (session-scoped, not tasks.sort_order); equal numbers = parallelizable.

payload.title: Add session next --tree showing task IDs in implementation order (tree-style, IDs only)
payload.description: A minimal session next --tree that renders do/plan tasks as a tree of IDs in implementation order with parallel groups (like tree for directories) - IDs only, no titles/legend/icons. Visualizes the order set by the session-ordering command; foundation for Claude later auto-running parallelizable tasks.

fields.text:
# Session-scoped task order + Claude-set sequencing

## Why
Mike repeatedly asks a session "what order should we implement these?" Capture that answer as data so `session next` shows the do/plan group in implementation order, with parallel groups visible — empowering him to run more in-flight tasks without losing track.

## Data
- Add `session_tasks.order` INTEGER (nullable). **Per-session** because ordering is session context — two sessions may reference the same task with different orders. `tasks.sort_order` is global and won't serve this; project-wide ordering is the separate future `project next`.
- Equal `order` values across tasks = **parallelizable** (safe to run concurrently).
- Migration rides with / after E-1462 (the other pending `session_tasks` column change).

## Command (Claude-callable)
- A verb that sets `session_tasks.order` for the current session.
- Compact spec: whitespace advances the order counter; `|` groups tasks at the SAME order (parallel):
  - `E-100 E-101|E-102 E-103` → E-100=1, E-101=2, E-102=2, E-103=3 (E-101 ∥ E-102).
- Accept JSON as an alternative input form for programmatic use.
- Scope: only the current session's `session_tasks` rows; unknown/foreign task ids rejected.

## Consumed by
- `session next`: sort the do/plan group by `order`; equal order renders as a parallel group.
- `session next --tree` (sibling task): renders the order/parallel structure.

## Out of scope
- Project-wide ordering (`project next`) — later.

# session next --tree — order/parallel structure, IDs only

## Why
The flat `session next` list can't show implementation order or which tasks are parallelizable. A `--tree` view makes the structure visible on demand, and is the foundation for later "Claude knows what it can safely run in parallel."

## Behavior
- `endless session next --tree` prints ONLY task IDs in their implementation order, tree-style (à la the `tree` command for directories).
- Initially no titles, legend, block icons, or other columns — keep it minimal.
- Order/parallel structure comes from `session_tasks.order` (sibling task): sequential orders stack; equal orders render as siblings within a parallel group.

## Depends on
- The session ordering data + command (sibling task).
- The `session next` read command itself (E-1465).

## Out of scope
- Titles / rich rendering in tree mode — possible later enhancement.

payload.title: Redefine ready to mean approved-to-spawn, plan optional
payload.description: Previously 'ready' implied an approved plan. Redefine 'ready' = approved to spawn, plan optional: a task reaches 'ready' either via a written+approved plan OR via Claude judging the description sufficient to spawn with user approval. session next's 'do' surfaces all 'ready' tasks regardless of whether a plan exists. Both paths still need user approval, but it can be lightweight: when the user asks whether a task is spawnable and Claude affirms, Claude may set 'ready' directly (a desired process, not an enforced gate). Whether approval is required at all becomes a future per-project approval-policy setting, out of scope here.

fields.text:
# session next --tree — order/parallel structure, IDs only

## Why
The flat `session next` list can't show implementation order or which tasks are parallelizable. A `--tree` view makes the structure visible on demand, and is the foundation for later "Claude knows what it can safely run in parallel."

## Behavior
- `endless session next --tree` prints ONLY task IDs in their implementation order, tree-style (à la the `tree` command for directories).
- Initially no titles, legend, block icons, or other columns — keep it minimal.
- Order/parallel structure comes from `session_tasks.order` (sibling task): sequential orders stack; equal orders render as siblings within a parallel group.

## Auto-derived order (key insight)
Order/parallel structure is largely **derivable from the blocked-by DAG**: a task's position = its depth in the `task_deps('blocks')` graph (topological layering); tasks at the same depth with no inter-dependency are parallelizable. So `--tree` can compute the structure automatically from existing blockers — the manual `session_tasks.order` (sibling task) is only needed to express parallelism the blockers don't imply, or to override. This means most "what order?" answers are already latent in the graph; the view surfaces them without Claude re-deriving each time.

## Depends on
- The session ordering data + command (sibling task) — for overrides / non-blocker parallelism.
- The `session next` read command itself (E-1465).

## Out of scope
- Titles / rich rendering in tree mode — possible later enhancement.

# E-1640 — Fix duplicate session rows minted per Claude launch when TMUX_PANE is empty

**Independent (parent of E-1641).** Full evidence in this task's `analysis` field. · **Layer:** Go

## Root cause (recap)
Dedup key is `sessions.session_id` (the Claude UUID), not `active_task_id`. Every launch gets a
new UUID, so SessionStart's upsert finds no conflict and **INSERTs a new row** via `TouchSession`
(`internal/monitor/session.go:236-295`, from `internal/hookcmd/claude.go:161`) and `InitSession`
(`session.go:104-121`). The only intended dedup — collision-invalidation by `process` (tmux pane),
`session.go:280-292` — is **inert** because `TMUX_PANE` is empty: `process` is NULL on 728/744
rows and `COALESCE(NULLIF(excluded.process,''))` refuses to store an empty pane. Sub-10s ghosts accrue.

## Files
- `internal/monitor/session.go`: `TouchSession` (220-295), `InitSession` (104-121), collision
  invalidation (280-292), `BindSessionToTask` (38-60).
- `internal/hookcmd/claude.go`: SessionStart ordering (154-180), `TouchSession` call (~161).

## Approach — active_task_id-scoped fallback dedup for the empty-pane case
When `process` is empty **and** `active_task_id` is known, fold into the existing recent
non-ended row for that task instead of inserting a fresh-UUID row:
- **Task known at SessionStart?** For spawned sessions yes (`@endless_task_id` window var read by
  the hook). Confirm binding order: if `BindSessionToTask` runs *after* `TouchSession`, move/peek
  the task id earlier or run the fallback on the bind step.
- **Fold rule:** if a non-ended row exists for the same `active_task_id` with empty `process` and
  recent `last_activity`, reuse it (update `session_id` + `last_activity`) rather than INSERT.
  Scope tightly (same task + empty process + recent) so two genuinely concurrent sessions aren't merged.
- **Ad-hoc (unbound) sessions:** task unknown → still create a row (real session until bound).

## Verification — create a per-task verify script
Create `tests/tasks/e-1640-verify.sh` in the `e-1624-verify.sh` shape (bash, `set -u`, the
`section`/`report_*`/`summary` helpers, `cd` repo root, ensure `go.work`, exit 0/1/2). Drive the
REAL code path via Go tests in `internal/monitor` — don't re-implement dedup logic.

The script asserts (named checks):
- `internal/monitor` compiles (`go build ./internal/monitor/`).
- A Go test (`go test -count=1 -run ^TestTouchSession_FoldsEmptyPaneSameTask$ ./internal/monitor/`)
  drives repeated `TouchSession` with empty `process` + the same `active_task_id` and asserts the
  row count stays **1** (folds), not N.
- A second Go test asserts a genuinely separate, freshly-claimed session on the same task is **NOT**
  folded into the stale row.
- `internal/monitor` full suite stays green (`go test -count=1 ./internal/monitor/`).

Re-runnability (sandbox/test DB):
- Tests build their own schema-applied DB and derive ids freshly — never hardcode a UNIQUE
  `session_id`/`short_id`; use fresh per-run values.
- Run the script at least TWICE before declaring it done.

## Implemented (2026-06-29) — decisions confirmed with Mike

The original "fold/reuse the row (update `session_id`)" wording above was **not**
used. Two decisions changed it:

1. **Mechanism = end stale rows, not mutate `session_id`.** Mutating a surviving
   row's `session_id` is FK-unsafe in production: `session_messages.session_id`
   REFERENCES `sessions(session_id)` ON DELETE CASCADE only (no ON UPDATE), so
   the update throws `FOREIGN KEY constraint failed` whenever the fold target has
   any messages — and there is no migration path to add ON UPDATE CASCADE
   (schema.sql `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` can't alter existing DBs; the
   V-migration framework was removed). Instead: keep the new-UUID row, and in
   `BindSessionToTask` end any OTHER non-ended foreground (`kind_id` = tmux) row
   for the same `active_task_id` that has no pane. FK-safe (only `state` changes),
   mirrors the existing collision-invalidation idiom.
2. **No recency window.** End all prior matching rows regardless of
   `last_activity` age. A resume can happen long after the prior row went idle, so
   a recency window would miss that duplicate; concurrency-merge is prevented by
   worktree locks (one live foreground session per task) plus the `kind_id` guard
   that excludes background agents.

**Code:** `internal/monitor/session.go` `BindSessionToTask` — bind upsert +
scoped invalidation now run in one transaction. All bind paths (spawn-marker
auto-bind, `autoBindFromCwd`, `StartWorkSession`) route through it.

**Tests:** `internal/monitor/session_dedup_test.go` —
`TestBindSessionToTask_EndsStalePanelessRowsSameTask`,
`_RepeatedLaunchesStayAtOneLiveRow`, `_DoesNotEndBackgroundAgentSameTask`,
`_DoesNotEndPanedRowSameTask`, `_DoesNotEndOtherTasksRows`. (Named for the actual
bind-step mechanism, not the plan's `TestTouchSession_Folds*`.)

**Verify-handoff — tell the user to verify with (only) this:**
```
esu && ./tests/tasks/e-1640-verify.sh
```

# E-1682 — Durable session-navigation trail (manual + goto) ## Context E-1681 shipped `session goto`/`back` with an intentionally ephemeral, goto-only back-stack (a tmux server option). While designing it, Mike noted the navigation *path* itself has durable value — usability analysis of how sessions get navigated, and recovering a session you lost track of. This task adds a separate, **durable** trail that captures *every* focus change (manual moves and `goto`), decoupled from `session back` (which stays goto-only and predictable). ## Decisions (resolved with Mike — implement as written, not as open choices) - **Single capture point.** A global tmux hook on focus-change events records every move. `session goto`'s `switch-client` is caught by the same hook, so goto carries no recording logic of its own. - **Events hooked:** `client-session-changed` and `session-window-changed` (moving between Claude sessions == changing window/session). Do **not** hook `pane-focus-in` (intra-window noise). - **`via` tag:** `session goto` sets a one-shot tmux server option `@endless_nav_via=goto` immediately before switching; the recorder reads + clears it, writing `via=goto`, else `via=manual`. This is the only change to landed E-1681 code. - **Scope/keying:** per tmux **client** (the navigator), matching the back-stack model. Edge endpoints are stored as session ids (resolved from the pane) plus the raw panes for untracked locations. - **Storage tier:** a new mutable runtime table in the local DB, written directly in Go — the **same non-committed tier as `sessions`**, NOT the committed JSONL ledger. High-churn nav state must never pollute the shared multi-dev ledger. - **Retention:** unbounded append in v1 (single-user, unshipped). Pruning is deferred and explicitly not built now. - **Read surface:** a minimal `endless session trail` viewer ships in v1. ## Data model New migration under `internal/schema/changes/` (a real `.sql` file — tool-openable; mirror an existing `changes/*.sql`). Follow the house schema conventions: no `TIMESTAMP` type (TEXT + ISO), FKs as separate clauses, `_id`-suffixed integer FK columns, and **no CHECK constraints** — the enum is an FK to a seeded values table. `session_navigations`: - `id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY` - `client TEXT NOT NULL` — tmux `client_name` (the navigator) - `project_id INTEGER` — FK `projects(id)` - `from_session_id INTEGER` — FK `sessions(id)`, nullable (first move / untracked) - `from_pane TEXT` — raw pane id when the source isn't a tracked session, nullable - `to_session_id INTEGER` — FK `sessions(id)`, nullable (untracked destination) - `to_pane TEXT NOT NULL` — the new focus pane - `via_id INTEGER NOT NULL` — FK `nav_via_kinds(id)` - `created_at TEXT NOT NULL` — ISO timestamp `nav_via_kinds(id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, slug TEXT)` seeded idempotently with `(1,'manual'), (2,'goto')`. Go side: the int-backed enum pattern (explicit numeric constants + `String()` + `ParseNavVia()`). ## Components (anchored by symbol — confirm exact sites at implement time) 1. **Schema migration** — `internal/schema/changes/e-1682-*.sql`, plus the seeded `nav_via_kinds` rows. Mirror the structure of a recent `changes/*.sql`. 2. **Recorder subcommand** — a new `endless-go` verb (mirror `internal/sessionquerycmd/` or `internal/tmuxcmd/`), e.g. `endless-go tmux record-nav --client --pane

`: - resolve `--pane` → session id + project (reuse the pane→session helpers in `internal/monitor/tmux_lookup.go`, e.g. `GetLiveSessionByProcess`, `GetActiveTaskForPane`). - read this client's most-recent row → use its `to_*` as the new `from_*`. - read + clear `@endless_nav_via` to set `via`. - write the row via the **sessions runtime-write path** (mirror `internal/monitor/session.go` / `internal/events/executor.go`), not the committed ledger. - keep it fast and silent — it fires on every focus change. 3. **Hook install** — extend `internal/tmuxcmd/apply.go` `buildApplySteps` to add `set-hook -g client-session-changed` and `set-hook -g session-window-changed` steps that `run-shell " tmux record-nav --client=#{client_name} --pane=#{pane_id}"`. Mirror the existing `run-shell` hook/binding steps already built there; they're re-applied idempotently wherever `apply` runs today. 4. **goto marker** — in `src/endless/session_cmd.py` `session_goto`, set `@endless_nav_via=goto` via tmux `set-option` immediately before `_tmux_switch_client`. One-shot; the recorder clears it. 5. **Viewer** — `endless session trail` (`cli.py` registration + `session_cmd.py` logic). Lists recent edges for the current client (resolve `client_name`), newest-first: `from → to` as session id + `task_id_display(active_task)` + summary, the `via` tag, and relative time. The DB read goes through Go (add a `session-query` reader; reads-in-Go rule — no new Python SQLite reads). Default = current client; `--all` for every client. ## Tests - **Go:** recorder resolves pane→session, forms `from→to` from the client's last row, reads/clears the `via` marker, and writes the row; migration applies and seeds `nav_via_kinds`. - **Python:** `session trail` rendering (mock the Go query) — newest-first, `via` tag, task labels, empty-state. - **`tests/tasks/e-1682-verify.sh`** (E-1681 model, non-disruptive): assert the migration/table exists; assert `apply` installs the nav hooks (check `tmux show-hooks -g`); drive the recorder against a synthetic pane on an **isolated** tmux server (`tmux -L …`) + sandbox DB and assert a row lands; assert `session trail` renders it; assert `goto` sets the marker and the recorder clears it. ## Verification - `just build`; `just test` green. - Manual, in tmux: switch between windows/sessions, run `endless session goto`, then `endless session trail` — edges appear with correct `via` tags and resolve to the right sessions/tasks. ## Out of scope - Pruning / retention policy (revisit only if the table grows). - pane-level (intra-window) focus capture. - Jumping to a trail entry (`goto` a row) — a possible later enhancement.

payload.title: Canonical tmux pane to session/task resolution is monitor.GetActiveTaskForPane
payload.description: Resolving which Endless session and task a tmux pane belongs to has ONE canonical implementation: monitor.GetActiveTaskForPane in internal/monitor/tmux_lookup.go. It tries the pane itself, then all panes in the same window via listPanesInSameWindow, and selects the session with the newest last_activity where state != 'ended' (NOT state in working/idle, which wrongly excludes bound and other live states) and active_task_id is not null. Established by E-1530 (ghost-row fix for reused pane ids after a tmux server restart). The status line and all future window-aware readers, including session next (E-1465), MUST call this resolver rather than re-deriving pane-to-session logic. The session-next prototype replicates it in shell only as throwaway scaffolding; the Go command must call GetActiveTaskForPane directly.

fields.text:
# Implement `endless session next` (the read command)

Build the real Go command behind the throwaway bash/sqlite prototype at `~/.config/endless/session-next.sql` + `session-next.sh`. **That prototype is the authoritative spec** for the query, columns, sorting, and rendering — read it first. The Go command supersedes and deletes both.

## Architecture
- `endless session next` lives in the Python `session` command group, delegating the read to **Go (endless-go)** per the reads-in-Go rule (E-1486): the Python verb shells out to an endless-go subcommand and prints its output. (Confirm the existing Python→endless-go delegation pattern, e.g. `session-query`.)
- **Focal-task resolution** — do NOT re-derive pane→session logic; compose the existing Go resolvers, matching claude.go's documented priority (*session active task > `@endless_task_id`*):
  1. `monitor.GetActiveTaskForPane($TMUX_PANE)` (ED-1523) — live session's `active_task` via process match (state != 'ended').
  2. **Fallback** when that returns nothing — which is COMMON: active windows are often state `needs_input` with NULL `active_task`, and bound sessions can be `ended` with empty `process`, so the process-based resolver finds neither. Fall back to the window's `@endless_task_id` via `monitor.tmuxTaskID` (claude.go:972; reads it with `tmux display-message -p "#{@endless_task_id}"`).
  3. Global most-recent only as a last resort.
  The bash prototype gets this wrong precisely because it can't call these Go functions — the real command must. (This extends ED-1523, which named `GetActiveTaskForPane` as canonical, with the `@endless_task_id` fallback for null-active windows.)

## Row set (per the prototype)
For focal task T (= the window session's `active_task_id` via GetActiveTaskForPane):
- `session_tasks` for ALL sessions whose `active_task_id = T` (cross-project; dup-session-proof — matches `WHERE active_task_id=T`)
- ∪ the focal task T
- ∪ the parent session's focal task (parent = this window's `@endless_spawned_by` source pane → its session)

Omit done-work (terminal status) EXCEPT the focal and parent rows, which always show. `--all` includes done-work.

## Columns (left→right), single-spaced
`<action-icon> <type-letter> <E-id> <phase-char> <block> <title-fills-width>`
- **action icon**: `●` this · `↑` parent · `⟳` doing (another live session on it) · `▶` do (ready + has text) · `✎` plan (unplanned/needs_plan) · `☑` verify · `◷` orphan (in_progress, no live session) · `·` other. (No `⏸` as an action.)
- **type letter**: E/T/B/R/Z from `task_types.slug`
- **phase char**: `!` urgent, `1` now, `2` next, `3` later, `?` maybe; `✓` when the task is terminal/done (focal/parent only)
- **block column** (auto-width 0/1/2): `⊗` blocked-by (any open blocker), `⏸` blocks (this blocks others). Width 0 if nothing blocked; 1 if no single task needs both; 2 only when some task is both.
- **title**: fills remaining terminal width (detect term size like `task list` does); truncate.

Use **display-width-aware rendering** (runewidth) — the prototype fights byte-vs-display width in `printf`; Go must pad by display width. Phase-by-intensity color via **lipgloss** is fine in Go (the prototype reverted raw ANSI as too fragile for bash).

## Sort
action-rank (this, parent, doing, do, plan, verify, orphan, other), then phase-rank (urgent<now<next<later<maybe), then id.

## Status model
`ready` = approved-to-spawn, plan optional (ED-1522); `do` (`▶`) surfaces ready tasks regardless of plan. Canonicalize status in-query (unplanned/needs_plan→plan; verify/unverified→verify; underway/in_progress→working; terminal = confirmed/assumed/declined/obsolete/completed).

## Out of scope (separate tasks)
- `--watch` / TUI — E-859 / E-1622 (lipgloss/bubbletea).
- `--tree` order view (E-1684) + session ordering (E-1683).
- goto/back navigation (E-1681).
- Duplicate session rows (E-1640) — resolver is dup-proof regardless.

## Reference
Prototype (same machine, read directly): `~/.config/endless/session-next.sql` (query + layout) and `~/.config/endless/session-next.sh` (resolution + watch loop).

# Session-scoped task order + Claude-set sequencing

## Why
Mike repeatedly asks a session "what order should we implement these?" Capture that answer as data so `session next` shows the do/plan group in implementation order, with parallel groups visible — empowering him to run more in-flight tasks without losing track.

## Data
- Add `session_tasks.order` INTEGER (nullable). **Per-session** because ordering is session context — two sessions may reference the same task with different orders. `tasks.sort_order` is global and won't serve this; project-wide ordering is the separate future `project next`.
- Equal `order` values across tasks = **parallelizable** (safe to run concurrently).
- Migration rides with / after E-1462 (the other pending `session_tasks` column change).

## Command (Claude-callable)
- A verb that sets `session_tasks.order` for the current session.
- Compact spec: whitespace advances the order counter; `|` groups tasks at the SAME order (parallel):
  - `E-100 E-101|E-102 E-103` → E-100=1, E-101=2, E-102=2, E-103=3 (E-101 ∥ E-102).
- Accept JSON as an alternative input form for programmatic use.
- Scope: only the current session's `session_tasks` rows; unknown/foreign task ids rejected.

## Consumed by
- `session next`: sort the do/plan group by `order`; equal order renders as a parallel group.
- `session next --tree` (sibling task): renders the order/parallel structure.

## Out of scope
- Project-wide ordering (`project next`) — later.

## Decisions (resolved 2026-06-29)
- **Column name:** `session_tasks.do_order` (not `order` — reserved word, would need quoting everywhere). Still distinct from global `tasks.sort_order`.
- **Verb:** `endless session order "<spec>"` — new subcommand under the existing `session` Click group, alongside `session status`.
- **Set semantics:** Replace-all. The spec is the complete ordering; any `session_tasks` row for the session NOT named in the spec has its `do_order` reset to NULL. Idempotent / re-runnable.
- **Event-sourced** (not a direct Python write): new event kind `session_tasks.ordered` emitted via `event_bridge.emit_event` → Go executor `execSessionTasksOrdered`, mirroring `session_status.recorded`. Honors the "no Python DB writes" policy. The order data is committed to the shared ledger like other session-scoped events (precedent: `session_status.recorded`), but — like `session_statuses`/`session_tasks` — is a live side-effect, NOT replayed by `rebuild-db`/`ProjectToTempDB`.
- **JSON input form:** array-of-groups, isomorphic to the compact spec — `[["E-100"],["E-101","E-102"],["E-103"]]`. Selected with `--json`.
- **Validation:** task ids must already have a `session_tasks` row for the current session (rejects unknown/foreign ids). Structural errors (malformed id, empty spec/group, duplicate id) are rejected Python-side before any event is emitted; membership errors are rejected in the Go executor (rolls back the DB mutation; the attempt's ledger line is retained as audit, consistent with the events-authoritative generic-update path).
- **`updated_at`:** reordering does NOT bump `session_tasks.updated_at` (it isn't task work; the reap-worktrees staleness clock keys on that column).

payload.title: Add --watch live refresh to endless session next
payload.description: Add a --watch flag to 'endless session next' that re-renders the what's-next view on an interval (the bash prototype's 2s loop in the now-deleted ~/.config/endless/session-next.sh, moved to /tmp/session-next.sh), redrawing only on change. E-1465 deliberately shipped the one-shot read; this is the deferred live-refresh mode. The plan pointed at E-859 (session watch <id> = conversation tail) and E-1622 (bg-agent TUI per epic), but neither owns THIS view's watch mode — hence this dedicated task. Decide watch mechanism (poll vs event-subscribe) and whether it stays a flag on the Go renderer or graduates to a lipgloss/bubbletea TUI (cf. E-859/E-1622) at plan time.
